name: Blenders
summary: Countertop blenders for smoothies, soups, and food prep.

evidence.claim: Horsepower (HP) claims on blenders are peak HP (brief-burst maximum draw) not running HP. Running HP is typically 40-60% of peak HP.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Peak figures; running HP is typically 40-60% of peak (E1). Wattage and HP claims don't directly translate to blending performance; blade design and jar shape matter.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the HP figure running or peak? Running HP is the fair comparator.

confabulationPatterns.reality: No defined standard. Vitamix and Blendtec commercial-line blenders are different products from their consumer lines; 'commercial-grade' on consumer models is marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is this an actual commercial-line blender, or a consumer blender with 'commercial-grade' marketing copy?

compatibilityQuestions: Primary use: smoothies (need 1+ running HP), food prep (jar shape matters), nut butters (need strong motor + tamper)?

repairability.notes: Vitamix and Blendtec have strong parts markets; cheap blenders are replace-don't-repair.
